summary: Tested NEW iteration features: (1) PWA manifest/icon/index.html, (2) Quest decomposition endpoint POST /api/quests/{id}/decompose, (3) Step toggle PATCH /api/quests/{id}/steps/{sid} with auto-complete + XP award, (4) Frontend Quests UI with decompose button + step checklist + level-up modal. All 10 backend pytest cases pass (100%). All UI flows verified end-to-end via Playwright on the public preview URL: legacy Valider button, toggle-steps expand/collapse, ticking all 5 steps triggers auto-complete, +300 XP toast renders, LevelUpModal appears (level 3, E-Rank).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py decompose endpoint correctly uses 404 for missing quest, 400 for completed quest, 500 if AI returns no steps. Steps get fresh UUIDs and done=False — clean.
_complete_quest_internal extracted helper is reused both by POST /quests/{id}/complete and the auto-complete branch in PATCH /steps — single source of truth for XP/level/skill bookkeeping. Good DRY.

toggle_step endpoint guards against double auto-complete by checking q['status']=='active' before invoking _complete_quest_internal. Verified: toggling a step OFF on an already-completed quest does NOT re-trigger completion (auto_completed=false). XP correctly awarded once and only once.
QuestOut.steps defaults to [] — backwards compatible for old quests without the field; verified GET /quests still returns 200 for pre-existing quests.
ai_system.SYSTEM_DECOMPOSE prompt explicitly constrains 3-5 steps, 2-15 minutes each, action verbs — well-engineered. AI returned 5 valid steps in our test (~5s response).
manifest.json: name/short_name/display/start_url/theme_color/icons all conform to PWA spec. icon.svg served with correct image/svg+xml content-type via the frontend proxy.

Frontend Quests.jsx: decompose() correctly opens the steps panel after success (setOpenSteps + load). toggleStep() detects res.auto_completed && res.completion and routes through handleLevelUpAndAchievements which triggers LevelUpModal — verified visually. togglingStep keys are `${qid}-${stepId}` which is unique enough.

Minor (non-blocking): the decompose button shows a small text-only inline 'Décomposition en cours...' spinner instead of a more prominent loading state — fine for a secondary action, but a 5-15s AI call could feel unresponsive on slow connections. Consider disabling the surrounding card or showing a skeleton.

Minor (non-blocking): when toggling a step OFF on a completed quest, backend correctly returns auto_completed=false and the quest stays completed, but the UI flow doesn't surface that semantics to the user (the step toggle visually flips but no toast). Acceptable since the quest is already in the 'Complétées' section.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (PWA manifest served, decompose flow E2E, auto-complete E2E, level-up modal verified, legacy complete button verified)

seed_data_creation: Used POST /api/reset then direct MongoDB insert (pymongo) to seed a profile + 2 active quests, bypassing the LLM-budget-fragile /api/profile/initiate. Approach documented in test_decompose_pwa.py fixture.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use direct mongo seeding (pymongo + MONGO_URL/DB_NAME) instead of /api/profile/initiate to avoid LLM budget cap. Decompose endpoint averages 5-15s — set Playwright timeouts >=30s. After decompose, the QuestCard switches from `decompose-quest-{id}` button to `toggle-steps-{id}` button; the steps-list is initially auto-opened (decompose() sets openSteps[id]=true). LevelUpModal correctly appears when toggling final step crosses a level threshold. xp_for_level: level 2 needs 100 XP, level 3 needs 400 XP — 450 XP gave us level 3 as expected.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All 12 features from the review request are working correctly: PWA manifest+icon+index meta tags, decompose 200/400/404, steps field default=[], step toggle 200/404, auto-complete + completion + XP award, legacy Valider button regression.
